mnemonics: Outside In: An exterior point is OUTSIDE the set. A boundary point is ON the fence (neither fully IN nor OUT).
keyPoints: An exterior point of a set A has a neighborhood (open ball) that does NOT intersect A at all.
A boundary point of a set A has neighborhoods that ALWAYS intersect both A and the complement of A.
The exterior of a set is the interior of its complement.
commonMistakes: Confusing exterior points with points that are just 'far away'; they must have a specific local property (a ball that avoids the set).
Assuming a boundary point must belong to the set; it can be in the set OR in its complement.
